task_completion.description: Open the dialog to import a 3D model
task_completion.visual_evidence: The DreamPlan interface shows an open file explorer or import dialog for adding a custom 3D model.

task_completion.description: Select the bunny object file from the Documents folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'bunny.obj' (or 'stanford-bunny.obj') is selected within the Documents folder in the import dialog.

task_completion.description: Place the imported model in the front yard
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stanford Bunny 3D model is visible in the 3D or 2D view, placed in the front yard area of the house. It must be clearly visible (at least 500 pixels).

task_completion.description: Scale the model to approximately 1 meter (3 feet) in height
task_completion.visual_evidence: The properties panel shows the model's height dimension set to ~1 meter / 3 feet, OR the model visually appears to be about half the height of a standard exterior door in the 3D view.
A 'Save Project' dialog is visible, or the user clicks the Save icon/menu item, or a status indicator confirms the project is saved.

integrity.description: Agent used the DreamPlan GUI to perform actions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with DreamPlan's menus, toolbars, and dialogs rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying project files.

integrity.description: The model is genuinely imported into the 3D environment
integrity.visual_evidence: The bunny appears as a 3D mesh integrated into the DreamPlan scene (e.g., showing proper perspective, shadows, or selection bounding boxes), not as a pasted 2D image overlay.

integrity.description: The project was saved through the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action is performed within the DreamPlan software interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 3D model is the famous Stanford Bunny (a 3D rabbit shape). The file in the system may be named 'stanford-bunny.obj' or 'bunny.obj'. The expected placement location is the 'front_yard', and the model must be clearly visible in the scene (minimum 500 pixels). To verify the 1-meter (3 feet) height requirement, look for dimension settings in the UI or estimate visually (e.g., roughly half the height of a standard exterior door).
